informal: If $P \triangleleft G$, $P$ a $p$-Sylow subgroup of $G$, prove that $\varphi(P) = P$ for every automorphism $\varphi$ of $G$.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_2_11_7 {G : Type*} [Group G] {p : ℕ} (_hp : Nat.Prime p)
  {P : Sylow p G} (hP : P.Normal) :
  Subgroup.Characteristic (P : Subgroup G)
reasoning: The informal statement asks to prove that for a normal p-Sylow subgroup P of G, every automorphism φ of G satisfies φ(P) = P. This is precisely the definition of a characteristic subgroup: invariance under all automorphisms of the ambient group. The Lean signature takes a Sylow p-subgroup P with hypothesis P.Normal and concludes Subgroup.Characteristic (P : Subgroup G), which by Mathlib's definition means P is fixed setwise by every automorphism of G. The prime hypothesis is included (as `_hp`), and the universal quantification over automorphisms is captured inside the Characteristic definition. Hence the Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal claim.
